title: Space Battle at Kuat
content:

corvette/Legends|CR90 corvettes]] *[[Sundered Heart/Legends|''Sundered Heart'']] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=*Moderate [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] casualties *Several ''Tartan''-class patrol [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]] *2 orbital TIE construction facilities *4 orbital [[Drydock/Legends|drydock]] facilities *Several [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttles]] *5 incomplete ''Imperial I''-class [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] *''Tyranny'' damaged *Entire complements and crews of destroyed [[Starship/Legends|ships]] and stations |casual2= |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|This is the Kuat shipyard. We have come under Rebel attack. Requesting assistance.''"
"''This is the ''Tyranny''. Your satellite defenses should be adequate to deal with any Rebel fleet. Activate them!|The captain of the Star Destroyer ''Tyranny'' refuses an Imperial shipyard officer assistance|}} The '''Space Battle at Kuat''' was an operation performed in [[0 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]] by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Raymus Antilles/Legends|Raymus Antilles]] to lure the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Tyranny]]'' away from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Fresia/Legends|Fresia]], where four prototype [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] were being stored. During the battle, five [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvettes]], including the [[Sundered Heart/Legends|''Sundered Heart'']], commanded by Antilles, raided the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]], destroying multiple unfinished [[Starship/Legends|ships]] while the Imperials scrambled to rally reinforcements. The mission was a success for the rebels, who withdrew into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] soon after the ''Tyranny'' arrived. ==Prelude== {{Quote|There's no way we're getting past that Star Destroyer. You'd think the Fresians would have warned us!''"
"''It's not their fault. The nationalization wasn't announced. The Emperor likes his surprises.''"
"''So what now?''"
"''If the ''Tyranny'' is here, it's not at it's normal station… and the ''Tyranny'' is normally stationed at…''"
"''Kuat! The Imperial shipyards?''"
"''Yes! I think we can get their attention…|The pilot of the ''Sundered Heart'', speaking with Captain Raymus Antilles|}} During the early stages of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] decided to [[Imperialization/Legends|nationalize]] the independent [[Incom Corporation/Legends|Incom Corporation]], as it had done with other organizations before. However, much of the design and research teams of Incom strongly disagreed with this decision, to the point where many of them decided to defect to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].{{DB|starship|xwing|X-wing starfighter}} It was through these defectors that the Rebellion learned of a facility on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Fresia/Legends|Fresia]] holding the four prototypes of Incom's newest [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] design: the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 starfighter]], later known more commonly as the "X-wing" fighter. In [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the Rebel Alliance, in dire need of a new starfighter design to replace the aging [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]] that were in common use throughout the Rebellion at the time, dispatched a force led by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Raymus Antilles/Legends|Raymus Antilles]] to secure the prototype X-wings from the Empire. However, when Antilles and his task force entered into the [[Fre'ji system]], they detected the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Tyranny]]'' in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] around Fresia. This caused a serious complication: with the ''Tyranny'' in orbit, the task force was unable to approach the planet. ==The battle== {{Quote|Kuat Shipyard to all Imperial units! We are under siege by significant Rebel forces! All ships in the area, respond! Emergency Code Zero!
"This is the ''Tyranny'' responding to Code Zero. Be aware that you are altering plans set by Lord Vader himself, and you will answer to him if this code as been invoked lightly."|The Imperial commander at Kuat's call for reinforcements being answered by the Commander of the ''Tyranny''|}} [[File:Kuat_Diversion.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Antilles's task force, in formation at the beginning of the battle.]] Antilles, in his modified [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]], the [[Sundered Heart/Legends|''Sundered Heart'']], led four flanking [[Corvette/Legends|corvettes]] to perform a hit-and-run raid on the orbital [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] of the planet [[Kuat/Legends|Kuat]] in order to lure the ''Tyranny'' from its new station at Fresia back to its usual station above Kuat. Imperial resistance at Kuat was light, and the Rebel corvettes easily defeated the defending [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] and [[Tartan-class patrol cruiser|''Tartan''-class patrol cruisers]]. Many orbital resource containers were strewn about the half completed [[Starship/Legends|starships]] there, and the Rebel corvettes targeted the containers, which then exploded and destroyed the surrounding shipyards.[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War'': Prima Official Game Guide]] The ''Tyranny'' originally refused several times to respond to distress calls from the shipyard's [[Unidentified Kuat communications officer|communications officer]]. When the Rebels destroyed the last shipyard, the officer activated [[Emergency Code Zero (Imperial)|Emergency Code Zero]]. In response, the ''Tyranny'' returned to Kuat to render aid to the Imperial defenders. The [[Unidentified Tyranny commander|commander]] of the ''Tyranny'' warned the Comm Officer that by invoking Code Zero, he had altered Vader's plans and will answer to him if the code was invoked lightly. The [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] came out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] with the [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] down, giving the small Rebel raiding force a chance to damage the [[Warship/Legends|warship]] before escaping. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|The Tyranny and its fleet will most likely remain at their post while it is being repaired. The devastation of Kuat Shipyard should prove to be a lengthy distraction.|Mon Mothma, after the battle|}} With the devastation of the Kuat shipyards and the damaging of the ''Tyranny'', the Rebels believed that Fresia would be left unprotected, allowing them to raid the Incom facility. However, before the ''Tyranny'' had returned to Kuat, it had deployed numerous sentry drones to lock down the Fre'ji [[Star system/Legends|system]] in its absence. When the Rebels detected this, it seemed certain that the mission to retrieve the prototype X-wings would have to be canceled. However, the [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] was able to devise a plan that would allow the Rebels to slip past the Imperial defenses. C-3PO's [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] counterpart, [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], would proceed to the planet [[Wayland/Legends|Wayland]], where he would steal the Imperial codes that would allow a Rebel team to enter the Fre'ji system undetected. Antilles agreed to this plan and immediately [[Battle of Wayland|dispatched]] both C-3PO and R2-D2 to Wayland with a large ground force to escort them. ==Behind the scenes== The Space Battle at Kuat is the first mission of the Rebel campaign in the [[2006]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|PC game]] ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]''. The mission is the first in a series of three missions that lead to the acquisition of T-65 X-wing starfighters for use by the Rebellion. The battle was first identified as the "Space Battle at Kuat" in the game's [[Star Wars: Empire at War: Prima Official Game Guide|official strategy guide]], published by [[Prima Games]]. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War'': Prima Official Game Guide]] {{1stm}} *[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption Expansion'': Prima Official Game Guide]] ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{GCW Fresia}} {{Interlang |de=Schlacht von Kuat (0 VSY) |es=Batalla espacial en Kuat }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kuat, Space Battle at}} [[Category:Battles of Kuat]] [[Category:Battles of the Fresian campaign]]
